Patch Set 13: Code-Review-1

(10 comments)

Patch Set 13:

It would be nice to get this into 2.11 and 2.10.x, since this is a problem that ZFS users have hit several times.

Patch Set 13:

(5 comments)

Patch Set 13: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 13: Code-Review-1

The mechanism for reserved space provided by ZFS, which works with any ZFS-based MDT regardless of Lustre version, allows all the same capabilities, does it not?

Patch Set 13:

Olaf, what is the ZFS space reservation mechanism you are referencing?  Is that the spa_slop_shift parameter you described in http://wiki.lustre.org/ZFS_MDT_ENOSPC_Recovery or is there something else?

Will the need for that workaround go away with patch https://review.whamcloud.com/26930 "LU-8856 osd: mark specific transactions netfree"?

If yes, then I guess we could abandon this patch, since virtually everyone is using ZFS >= 0.6.5 these days with Lustre (and definitely more than the number of systems that will get this still-unlanded patch first).

Patch Set 13:

0.6.5 doesn't expose that interface to control netfree. see dmu_tx_mark_netfree() and netfree argument to dsl_dir_tempreserve_space() in 0.7

Patch Set 13:

> Olaf, what is the ZFS space reservation mechanism you are
 > referencing?

Sorry that was vague.  I'm referring to spa_slop_shift and the procedure I described on the wiki.

I'm arguing that this patch, "reserve space in zfs pool for emergency", essentially duplicates what zfs already provides with spa_slop_shift, and we should just direct administrators to use spa_slop_shift if their MDT fills up.

 > Will the need for that workaround go away with patch
 > https://review.whamcloud.com/26930 "LU-8856 osd: mark specific
 > transactions netfree"?

Some other transactions may need to be addressed.  For example, config logs written at MDT start time.  In my most recent case of a filled-up MDT I was unable to mount the dataset because of this.

But even with just what Alex has already done in the "mark specific transactions netfree" patch, I suspect the problem will be mostly addressed.  If the test in this patch were moved to the netfree patch, I think this patch could be abandoned.

Abandoned

Abandon in favour of https://review.whamcloud.com/26930 and http://wiki.lustre.org/ZFS_MDT_ENOSPC_Recovery

commit.subject: LU-8856 osd: reserve space in zfs pool for emergency
commit.message:
LU-8856 osd: reserve space in zfs pool for emergency

upon mount osd-zfs should reserve some fraction of space (using
writes initially) so that in case of ENOSPC a user has a chance
to release that space (rm with direct mount or using procfs) and
cleanup some space.

the space is reserved when a threshold for free space is met (it
is hardcoded at the moment). a special thread is introduced which
job is to watch this threshold and do reservation.

the reserved space can be released with:
  lctl set_param -n osd*.*MDT*.release_reserve=1

Test-Parameters: fortestonly ostfilesystemtype=zfs mdtfilesystemtype=zfs envdefinitions=SLOW=yes testlist=sanity

Change-Id: I37c5fe97a6715b2b7232f54f289233a00f08304a
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
